# Audiobook Speed Calculator

A free, lightweight audiobook speed calculator built with HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and Jekyll for GitHub Pages.

The calculator helps users determine how long an audiobook will take to finish at different playback speeds, including 1x, 1.25x, 1.5x, 2x, and higher.

## Features

* Calculate listening time at custom playback speeds
* Mobile-friendly responsive design
* Fast-loading static site
* SEO-optimized for search engines and AI search systems
* Jekyll-powered GitHub Pages deployment
* Structured data (FAQ schema and metadata)
* Automatically generated sitemap
* Topic-cluster content pages for audiobook-related searches

## Demo

After deployment, the calculator will be available at:

```text
https://yourusername.github.io/audiobook-speed-calculator/
```

Replace `yourusername` with your GitHub username.

## How It Works

The calculator uses a simple formula:

```text
Listening Time = Original Duration ÷ Playback Speed
```

### Example

| Original Length | Playback Speed | Actual Listening Time |
| --------------- | -------------- | --------------------- |
| 20 hours        | 1x             | 20 hours              |
| 20 hours        | 1.25x          | 16 hours              |
| 20 hours        | 1.5x           | 13 hours 20 minutes   |
| 20 hours        | 2x             | 10 hours              |
| 20 hours        | 3x             | 6 hours 40 minutes    |

## Deployment

This project is designed to be hosted using GitHub Pages.

### Enable GitHub Pages

1. Push the repository to GitHub.
2. Open **Settings → Pages**.
3. Select the branch containing the site.
4. Save the settings.
5. Wait for GitHub Pages to build and publish the site.

## SEO Features

This project includes several search engine optimization best practices:

### Technical SEO

* Jekyll SEO Tag
* Jekyll Sitemap
* robots.txt
* Canonical URLs
* Open Graph metadata
* Mobile-friendly layout
* Optimized images

### Content SEO

* Unique page titles
* Unique meta descriptions
* Structured heading hierarchy
* Internal linking
* Topic-cluster content strategy
* FAQ content

### AI Search Readiness

* Structured data
* FAQ schema
* Clear factual content
* Tables and lists for easy extraction
* Author and contact pages
* Human-readable content organization

## Planned Features

Future enhancements may include:

* Podcast speed calculator

* Dark mode support
* User-configurable speed presets
* Additional structured data schemas
